expectations.evidence: The reply presents items as "1. ... 2. ... 3. ... 8." — a numbered progression, not bullet points.
expectations.text: The answer is an ordered or numbered sequence of questions, not a flat unordered list.

expectations.evidence: Question 1 is "Where do you feel it — is it the whole app, or specific pages/actions?" (scope-level) versus question 8 "Can you walk me through the exact steps right before it slows down, one more time — I want to try it myself" (a concrete reproduction request), clearly narrower.
expectations.text: The first question is broader than the last — it opens wide, then narrows.

expectations.evidence: There are 8 numbered questions plus a closing catch-all question, well over four.
expectations.text: There are at least four questions.

expectations.evidence: Question 6 bundles two distinct questions into one numbered item: "Is this new, or has it always been this way? Anything change recently — an update, more data, a different device?" — this asks about regression status and about recent changes as separate ideas within a single list entry.
expectations.text: Each question carries a single idea.
